items.text:
🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[✨ ENRICHED\]

*Name:* Picton Castle
*Country:* wales
*Region:* west wales
*County:* Pembrokeshire
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 13th century
*Coords:* 51.784, -4.885
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Picton_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Picton%20Castle/@51.7833,-4.8667,15z
*Main Image:* https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/place-photos/AJRVUZMMefXJYknofWMQqzyqj17sLFfB_emfwPiBCrVsDMCxhR6RWE3fEyWMtfKN68aC-FQHo6NMkckiZ8ZBzSDj8_IDgEUaVVSpCc8_JGXSnErXLcp8YmtvQkqPnpLAV4c0k2gs2GUtzeey9pWprHa_QZbPcA=s4800-w1200
*Gallery:* 0 images
*Image Source:* google
*Has Wikipedia:* unknown
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* change google places link - https://maps.app.goo.gl/jgFsoopMCjmu74GMA
add source - https://pictoncastle.co.uk/

*Description:*
Picton Castle is a medieval castle near Haverfordwest in the community of Uzmaston, Boulston and Slebech, Pembrokeshire, Wales. Originally built at the end of the 13th century by a Flemish knight, it later came into the hands of Sir John Wogan. The castle and gardens are now owned and managed by the Picton Castle Trust, a registered charity, for the benefit of the public. It is of unusual construction and has been remodelled several times during its history. The castle is a Grade I listed building and its gardens and park are designated at Grade II* on the Cadw/ICOMOS Register of Parks and Gardens of Special Historic Interest in Wales.

_Listing 2 of 94_

*Country:* wales

*Period:* 16th century
*Coords:* 51.879511, -4.062723
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aberglasney_Gardens
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Aberglasney%20House/@51.856,-4.028,15z
*Main Image:* https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/73/Aberglasney_House_-_geograph.org.uk_-_536730.jpg/330px-Aberglasney_House_-_geograph.org.uk_-_536730.jpg

*Enrich Notes:* Change name to - Aberglasney
Update wikipedia page - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aberglasney
Add source - https://aberglasney.org/

*Description:*
Aberglasney House and Gardens is a medieval house and gardens set in the Tywi valley in the parish of Llangathen, Carmarthenshire, West Wales. It is owned and run by Aberglasney Restoration Trust, a registered charity. The house is a Grade II* listed building and the gardens are listed, also at Grade II*, on the Cadw/ICOMOS Register of Parks and Gardens of Special Historic Interest in Wales.

_Listing 3 of 94_

listing.name: Aberglasney House
listing.desc: Aberglasney House and Gardens is a medieval house and gardens set in the Tywi valley in the parish of Llangathen, Carmarthenshire, West Wales. It is owned and run by Aberglasney Restoration Trust, a registered charity. The house is a Grade II* listed building and the gardens are listed, also at Grade II*, on the Cadw/ICOMOS Register of Parks and Gardens of Special Historic Interest in Wales.

imageAttribution.description: Aberglasney House Viewed from the now restored Cloister Garden, the exterior of the house now looks in good shape. Inside, there is still a lot of restoration work going on.
